title: Bug analysis with automatic tracing
story: As a developer with a bug ticket, I want to say 'analyze this' and have the system understand it's a bug, gather context from the ticket and codebase, confirm its understanding with me, and then fix it autonomously with tracing -- so that I don't have to go through irrelevant requirements/architecture/design analysis for bugs.
acceptance_criteria: System infers from description content that the subject is a bug and confirms with me
System gathers bug context from ticket and scans codebase for relevant files
System plays back structured understanding: what's broken, where in code, what's affected
I can add more context or confirm the understanding is complete
System asks 'Should I fix it?' before launching any workflow
On confirmation, fix workflow runs from Phase 02 (tracing) autonomously with live progress

title: Override bug classification to feature
story: As a developer, when the system incorrectly classifies my item as a bug, I want to say 'no, it's a feature' and have the system fall through to the standard roundtable -- so that mislabeled items don't get stuck in the wrong analysis flow.
acceptance_criteria: I can override the LLM's bug classification
System routes to the standard roundtable after my override
No bug-gather artifacts are produced when I override

title: Analyze bug without fixing
story: As a developer, I want to analyze a bug to understand it without immediately fixing it -- so that I can review the analysis and decide to fix later.
acceptance_criteria: After playback, system asks 'Should I fix it?'
I can decline and the artifacts (bug-report.md, requirements-spec.md) are preserved
I can later invoke 'fix' and the workflow auto-detects existing artifacts, starting from Phase 02
